rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
Cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in modules.php in Php-Nuke 7.1.0 allows remote attackers to inject arbitrary web script or HTML via the (1) Your Name field, (2) e-mail field, (3) nicname field, (4) fname parameter, (5) ratenum parameter, or (6) search field.

Impact:
CVSS base score 4.3
CVSS impact score 2.9
CVSS exploitability score 8.6
confidentialityImpact NONE
integrityImpact PARTIAL
availabilityImpact PARTIAL

Details:

Ease of Attack:
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"SERVER-WEBAPP modules.php access"; flow:to_server,established; http_uri; content:"/modules.php",fast_pattern,nocase; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; reference:bugtraq,9879; reference:cve,2004-1817; classtype:web-application-activity; sid:2565; rev:14; )

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-WEBAPP -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in web based applications on servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
Buffer overflows in various CGI programs in the remote administration service for Trend Micro Interscan VirusWall 3.01 allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands.

Impact:
CVSS base score 10.0
CVSS impact score 10.0
CVSS exploitability score 10.0
confidentialityImpact COMPLETE
integrityImpact COMPLETE
availabilityImpact COMPLETE

Details:

Ease of Attack:
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET 1812 ( msg:"SERVER-WEBAPP VirusWall catinfo access"; flow:to_server,established; content:"/catinfo",nocase; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; reference:bugtraq,2579; reference:bugtraq,2808; reference:cve,2001-0432; reference:nessus,10650; classtype:attempted-recon; sid:1232; rev:14; )

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-WEBAPP -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in web based applications on servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation: This rule looks for attempts to exploit a directory traversal vulnerability in Compaq Insight.
rule_documentation.what_to_look_for: This rule looks for attempts to exploit a directory traversal vulnerability in Compaq Insight.
rule_documentation.rule_vulnerability:
Directory Traversal
 

 A Directory Traversal attack targets HTTP traffic and allows the attacker to access directories outside the applications own, potentially exposing sensitive system files to leakage or overwriting. This is also known as Directory Climbing, Path Traversal, or Backtracking. An alert on this kind of attack indicates a vulnerability in security validation of user input that allows a "traverse to parent directory" or "../" command to pass through. Protect your site by filtering all user input, removing any characters but the allowed data.
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET 2301 ( msg:"SERVER-WEBAPP Compaq Insight directory traversal"; flow:to_server,established; content:"../"; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; reference:bugtraq,282; reference:cve,1999-0771; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1199; rev:18; )

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-WEBAPP -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in web based applications on servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
Allaire JRun 2.3.3, 3.0 and 3.1 running on IIS 4.0 and 5.0, iPlanet, Apache, JRun web server (JWS), and possibly other web servers allows remote attackers to read arbitrary files and directories by appending (1) "%3f.jsp", (2) "?.jsp" or (3) "?" to the requested URL.

Impact:
CVSS base score 5.0
CVSS impact score 2.9
CVSS exploitability score 10.0
confidentialityImpact PARTIAL
integrityImpact NONE
availabilityImpact NONE

Details:

Ease of Attack:
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"SERVER-WEBAPP jrun directory browse attempt"; flow:to_server,established; http_uri; content:"/?.jsp"; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; reference:bugtraq,3592; reference:cve,2001-1510; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1376; rev:13; )

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-OTHER -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in a server in the network.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
Microsoft Personal Web Server and FrontPage Personal Web Server in some Windows systems allows a remote attacker to read files on the server by using a nonstandard URL.

Impact:
CVSS base score 5.0
CVSS impact score 2.9
CVSS exploitability score 10.0
confidentialityImpact PARTIAL
integrityImpact NONE
availabilityImpact NONE

Details:

Ease of Attack:
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"SERVER-OTHER Microsoft Frontpage .... request"; flow:to_server,established; http_uri; content:"..../"; metadata:policy max-detect-ips drop,ruleset community; service:http; reference:bugtraq,989; reference:cve,1999-0386; reference:cve,2000-0153; reference:nessus,10142; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:966; rev:25; )

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-WEBAPP -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in web based applications on servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
Impact:
Information gathering and system integrity compromise. Possible unauthorized
administrative access to the server. Possible execution of arbitrary code of 
the attackers choosing in some cases.

Details:
This event is generated when an attempt is made to compromise a host
running a Web server or a vulnerable application on a web server.

Many known vulnerabilities exist for each implementation and the 
attack scenarios are legion.

Some applications do not perform stringent checks when validating the
credentials of a client host connecting to the services offered on a
host server. This can lead to unauthorized access and possibly escalated
privileges to that of the administrator. Data stored on the machine can
be compromised and trust relationships between the victim server and 
other hosts can be exploited by the attacker.

Ease of Attack:
Simple. Exploits exist.
rule_documentation.what_to_look_for:
This event is generated when an attempt is made to exploit a known 
vulnerability on a web server or a web application resident on a web
server.
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"SERVER-WEBAPP /.... access"; flow:to_server,established; content:"/...."; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; classtype:attempted-recon; sid:1142; rev:11; )

rule_documentation.msg: SERVER-IIS Microsoft Windows IIS directory traversal attempt
rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-IIS -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in Microsoft IIS Web Servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
This event is generated when an attacker attempts to exploit a directory traversal vulnerability in Microsoft Office

Impact:
Information disclosure
Remote code execution

Details:
The rule is looking for the exploitation of the vulnerability when attempting to do directory traversal. 

Ease of Attack:
rule_documentation.what_to_look_for: This rule looks for attempts to trigger a directory traversal vulnerability in Microsoft Windows IIS servers.
rule_documentation.rule_vulnerability:
Directory Traversal
 

 A Directory Traversal attack targets HTTP traffic and allows the attacker to access directories outside the applications own, potentially exposing sensitive system files to leakage or overwriting. This is also known as Directory Climbing, Path Traversal, or Backtracking. An alert on this kind of attack indicates a vulnerability in security validation of user input that allows a "traverse to parent directory" or "../" command to pass through. Protect your site by filtering all user input, removing any characters but the allowed data.
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"SERVER-IIS Microsoft Windows IIS directory traversal attempt"; flow:to_server,established; content:"..|5C|..",fast_pattern,nocase; metadata:policy max-detect-ips drop,ruleset community; service:http; reference:bugtraq,2218; reference:cve,1999-0229; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:974; rev:23; )

rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
iChat ROOMS Webserver allows remote attackers to read arbitrary files via a .. (dot dot) attack.

Impact:
CVSS base score 5.0
CVSS impact score 2.9
CVSS exploitability score 10.0
confidentialityImpact PARTIAL
integrityImpact NONE
availabilityImpact NONE

Details:

Ease of Attack:
rule_documentation.what_to_look_for: This rule alerts on a directory traversal attempt on an iChat ROOMS Webserver.
rule_documentation.rule_vulnerability:
Directory Traversal
 

 A Directory Traversal attack targets HTTP traffic and allows the attacker to access directories outside the applications own, potentially exposing sensitive system files to leakage or overwriting. This is also known as Directory Climbing, Path Traversal, or Backtracking. An alert on this kind of attack indicates a vulnerability in security validation of user input that allows a "traverse to parent directory" or "../" command to pass through. Protect your site by filtering all user input, removing any characters but the allowed data.
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET 4080 ( msg:"SERVER-WEBAPP iChat directory traversal attempt"; flow:to_server,established; content:"/../../"; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; reference:cve,1999-0897; classtype:web-application-activity; sid:1604; rev:12; )

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-WEBAPP -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in web based applications on servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation: This rule looks for attempts to exploit a directory traversal vulnerability in Compaq Insight.
rule_documentation.what_to_look_for: This rule looks for attempts to exploit a directory traversal vulnerability in Compaq Insight.
rule_documentation.rule_vulnerability:

 A Directory Traversal attack targets HTTP traffic and allows the attacker to access directories outside the applications own, potentially exposing sensitive system files to leakage or overwriting. Protect your site by filtering all user input, removing any characters but the allowed data.
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET 2301 ( msg:"SERVER-WEBAPP Compaq Insight directory traversal"; flow:to_server,established; content:"../"; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; reference:bugtraq,282; reference:cve,1999-0771; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1199; rev:18; )

rule_documentation.rule_vulnerability:
Directory Traversal
 

 A Directory Traversal attack targets HTTP traffic and allows the attacker to access directories outside the applications own, potentially exposing sensitive system files to leakage or overwriting. This is also known as Directory Climbing, Path Traversal, or Backtracking. An alert on this kind of attack indicates a vulnerability in security validation of user input that allows a "traverse to parent directory" or "../" command to pass through. Protect your site by filtering all user input, removing any characters but the allowed data.

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-WEBAPP -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in web based applications on servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
This event is generated when an attempt is made to access the file 
".htaccess" from a web server.

Impact:
If this request is successful, it could provide an attacker with 
valuable information needed to compromise the website.

Details:
Most UNIX based web servers, such as Apache and Netscape Enterprise 
Server, use ".htaccess" files to customize security settings on a 
per-directory level. These files can specify things like what users 
have access to what resources, hosts that are allowed or denied, and 
what type of authentication system to use. This type of data would be 
most useful for carrying out an attack on the site. Fortunately, all 
modern web servers deny client access to these files by default.

Ease of Attack:
It is simple to send a request for this file, but the request would only
be successful if the file exists and the server allows access to it.
rule_documentation.what_to_look_for:
This rule triggers when an attempt is made to access the file 
".htaccess" from a web server.
rule_documentation.false_positives:
Known false positives, with the described conditions

While unlikely, certain web servers that are set up to host multiple 
users' sites may allow access to this file by the site owners.
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"SERVER-WEBAPP .htaccess access"; flow:to_server,established; http_uri; content:".htaccess",fast_pattern,nocase; metadata:policy max-detect-ips drop,ruleset community; service:http; reference:url,attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1170; classtype:attempted-recon; sid:1129; rev:16; )

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-WEBAPP -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in web based applications on servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
Impact:
Information gathering and system integrity compromise. Possible unauthorized
administrative access to the server. Possible execution of arbitrary code of 
the attackers choosing in some cases.

Details:
This event is generated when an attempt is made to compromise a host
running a Web server or a vulnerable application on a web server.

Many known vulnerabilities exist for each implementation and the 
attack scenarios are legion.

Some applications do not perform stringent checks when validating the
credentials of a client host connecting to the services offered on a
host server. This can lead to unauthorized access and possibly escalated
privileges to that of the administrator. Data stored on the machine can
be compromised and trust relationships between the victim server and 
other hosts can be exploited by the attacker.

Ease of Attack:
Simple. Exploits exist.
rule_documentation.what_to_look_for:
This event is generated when an attempt is made to exploit a known 
vulnerability on a web server or a web application resident on a web
server.
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"SERVER-WEBAPP /.... access"; flow:to_server,established; content:"/...."; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; classtype:attempted-recon; sid:1142; rev:11; )

rule_documentation.rule_vulnerability:
Directory Traversal
 

 A Directory Traversal attack targets HTTP traffic and allows the attacker to access directories outside the applications own, potentially exposing sensitive system files to leakage or overwriting. This is also known as Directory Climbing, Path Traversal, or Backtracking. An alert on this kind of attack indicates a vulnerability in security validation of user input that allows a "traverse to parent directory" or "../" command to pass through. Protect your site by filtering all user input, removing any characters but the allowed data.

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-IIS -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in Microsoft IIS Web Servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
This event is generated when an attempt is made to exploit a potential weakness on a host running Microsoft Internet Information Server (IIS). 

Impact:
Information gathering possible administrator access.

Details:
This event indicates that an attempt has been made to exploit potential weaknesses in a host running Microsoft IIS.

The attacker may be trying to gain information on the IIS implementation on the host, this may be the prelude to an attack against that host using that information.

The attacker may also be trying to gain administrator access to the host, garner information on users of the system or retrieve sensitive customer information.

Some applications may store sensitive information such as database connections, user information, passwords and customer information in files accessible via a web interface. Care should be taken to ensure these files are not accessible to external sources.

Ease of Attack:
Simple.
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"SERVER-IIS /scripts/samples/ access"; flow:to_server,established; http_uri; content:"/scripts/samples/",nocase; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; reference:nessus,10370; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1400; rev:18; )

rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"OS-MOBILE Android User-Agent detected"; flow:to_server,established; http_header; content:"User-Agent|3A|"; content:"android",distance 0,fast_pattern,nocase; pcre:"/^User-Agent\x3a[^\r\n]*android/i"; metadata:policy max-detect-ips drop,ruleset community; service:http; classtype:policy-violation; sid:25521; rev:4; )

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-WEBAPP -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in web based applications on servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
The undocumented TRACK method in Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS) 5.0 returns the content of the original request in the body of the response, which makes it easier for remote attackers to steal cookies and authentication credentials, or bypass the HttpOnly protection mechanism, by using TRACK to read the contents of the HTTP headers that are returned in the response, a technique that is similar to cross-site tracing (XST) using HTTP TRACE.

Impact:
CVSS base score 5.8
CVSS impact score 4.9
CVSS exploitability score 8.6
confidentialityImpact PARTIAL
integrityImpact PARTIAL
availabilityImpact PARTIAL

Details:

Ease of Attack:
rule_documentation.what_to_look_for: The undocumented TRACK method in Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS) 5.0 returns the content of the original request in the body of the response, which makes it easier for remote attackers to steal cookies and authentication credentials, or bypass the HttpOnly protection mechanism, by using TRACK to read the contents of the HTTP headers that are returned in the response, a technique that is similar to cross-site tracing (XST) using HTTP TRACE.
rule_documentation.rule_vulnerability:
Authentication Bypass
 

 An Authentication Bypass occurs when there is a way to avoid providing user credentials to a system before performing restricted operations on said system.
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"SERVER-WEBAPP TRACE attempt"; flow:to_server,established; content:"TRACE",depth 5; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; reference:bugtraq,9561; reference:cve,2003-1567; reference:cve,2004-2320; reference:cve,2010-0360; reference:nessus,11213; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:2056; rev:12; )

alert.alert_message: OS-OTHER Bash CGI environment variable injection attempt

rule_documentation.msg: OS-OTHER Bash CGI environment variable injection attempt
rule_documentation.rule_category: OS-OTHER -- Snort has detected traffic targeting vulnerabilities in a non-standard operating system (not Windows, Linux, Solaris, or mobile). This does not include browser traffic or other software on the OS, but attacks against the OS itself.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
GNU Bash through 4.3 processes trailing strings after function definitions in the values of environment variables, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted environment, as demonstrated by vectors involving the ForceCommand feature in OpenSSH sshd, the mod_cgi and mod_cgid modules in the Apache HTTP Server, scripts executed by unspecified DHCP clients, and other situations in which setting the environment occurs across a privilege boundary from Bash execution, aka "ShellShock." NOTE: the original fix for this issue was incorrect; CVE-2014-7169 has been assigned to cover the vulnerability that is still present after the incorrect fix.

Impact:
CVSS base score 10.0
CVSS impact score 10.0
CVSS exploitability score 10.0
confidentialityImpact COMPLETE
integrityImpact COMPLETE
availabilityImpact COMPLETE

Details:

Ease of Attack:
rule_documentation.what_to_look_for: This rule alerts when attempts to exploit CVE-2014-6278 and CVE-2014-7169 are detected
rule_documentation.rule_vulnerability:
Command Injection
 

 Command Injection attacks target applications that allow unsafe user-supplied input. Attackers transmit this input via forms, cookies, HTTP headers, etc. and exploit the applications permissions to execute system commands without injecting code.
rule_documentation.metadata: policy balanced-ips drop,policy max-detect-ips drop,policy security-ips drop,ruleset community
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"OS-OTHER Bash CGI environment variable injection attempt"; flow:to_server,established; http_header; content:"() {",fast_pattern,nocase; metadata:policy balanced-ips drop,policy max-detect-ips drop,policy security-ips drop,ruleset community; service:http; reference:cve,2014-6271; reference:cve,2014-6277; reference:cve,2014-6278; reference:cve,2014-7169; classtype:attempted-admin; sid:31978; rev:5; )

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-WEBAPP -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in web based applications on servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
This event is generated when an attempt is made to exploit a known 
vulnerability on a web server or a web application resident on a web
server.

Impact:
Information gathering and system integrity compromise. Possible unauthorized
administrative access to the server. Possible execution of arbitrary code of 
the attackers choosing in some cases.

Details:
This event is generated when an attempt is made to compromise a host
running a Web server or a vulnerable application on a web server.

Many known vulnerabilities exist for each implementation and the 
attack scenarios are legion.

Some applications do not perform stringent checks when validating the
credentials of a client host connecting to the services offered on a
host server. This can lead to unauthorized access and possibly escalated
privileges to that of the administrator. Data stored on the machine can
be compromised and trust relationships between the victim server and 
other hosts can be exploited by the attacker.

Ease of Attack:
Simple. Exploits exist.
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"SERVER-WEBAPP backup access"; flow:to_server,established; http_uri; content:"/backup",fast_pattern,nocase; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; classtype:attempted-recon; sid:1213; rev:13; )

rule_documentation.rule_category: SERVER-IIS -- Snort has detected traffic exploiting vulnerabilities in Microsoft IIS Web Servers.
rule_documentation.rule_explanation:
This event is generated when an attempt is made to exploit a potential weakness on a host running Microsoft Internet Information Server (IIS). 

Impact:
Information gathering possible administrator access.

Details:
This event indicates that an attempt has been made to exploit potential weaknesses in a host running Microsoft IIS.

The attacker may be trying to gain information on the IIS implementation on the host, this may be the prelude to an attack against that host using that information.

This rule generates an event when an attempt is made to browse the scripts directory on a web server. This driectory may contain source code files for web application scripts running on the server. An attacker may be able to view the source code and use the information in further attacks against the server.

Ease of Attack:
Simple.
rule_documentation.rule_text: alert tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"SERVER-IIS scripts-browse access"; flow:to_server,established; content:"/scripts/ ",fast_pattern,nocase; metadata:ruleset community; service:http; reference:nessus,11032; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1029; rev:18; )
